questions.text: Which Mughal emperor was told by the British that his family would have to move out of the Red Fort after his death?
questions.explanation: In 1849, Governor-General Dalhousie announced this major decision.

questions.text: In 1856, Governor-General ________ decided that Bahadur Shah Zafar would be the last Mughal king.
questions.explanation: Canning declared that his descendants would only be recognized as princes.

questions.text: Nana Sahib was the adopted son of:
questions.explanation: He pleaded that he be given his father's pension when the latter died, but the Company refused.

questions.text: The British passed laws to stop the practice of ________ and to encourage the remarriage of ________.
questions.explanation: These social reforms were seen by many Indians as an interference in their religion and customs.

questions.text: In 1850, a new law was passed to make conversion to ________ easier.
questions.explanation: The law allowed an Indian who converted to inherit the property of his ancestors.

questions.text: What was the main grievance of the sepoys regarding the new cartridges?
options: They were greased with the fat of cows and pigs
questions.explanation: Soldiers had to bite the cartridges, which hurt the religious sentiments of both Hindus and Muslims.

questions.text: Who was the young soldier hanged on 29 March 1857 for attacking his officers in Barrackpore?
questions.explanation: His execution sparked widespread anger among the sepoys.

questions.text: The rebellion of 1857 began from which city on 10th May?
questions.explanation: The sepoys of Meerut marched to Delhi after revolting against their officers.

questions.text: Whom did the mutinous sepoys proclaim as the leader of the rebellion in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Though old and reluctant, the Mughal Emperor became the symbolic head of the revolt.

questions.text: Where did Nana Sahib lead the revolt?
questions.explanation: He expelled the British garrison from the city and proclaimed himself Peshwa.

questions.text: Who led the rebellion in Lucknow after her son Birjis Qadr was proclaimed Nawab?
questions.explanation: She took an active part in organizing the uprising against the British.

questions.text: Rani Lakshmibai joined the rebel sepoys and fought the British along with ________.
questions.explanation: Tantia Tope was the general of Nana Sahib who joined forces with the Rani.

questions.text: Who was the old zamindar from Bihar who joined the rebel sepoys and fought the British?
questions.explanation: Despite his age, Kunwar Singh was a brilliant military leader of the revolt.

questions.text: Who was Bakht Khan?
options: A soldier from Bareilly who took charge of a large force in Delhi
questions.explanation: He became a key military figure in the defense of Delhi.

questions.text: How did the British suppress the rebellion in Delhi?
options: They recaptured Delhi in September 1857 and arrested Bahadur Shah Zafar
They asked the French for help
questions.explanation: Delhi was the first major city to be retaken by the British.

questions.text: What happened to Bahadur Shah Zafar after the revolt was crushed?
options: He was sent to life imprisonment in Rangoon
questions.explanation: He and his wife were sent to Rangoon, where he died in 1862.

questions.text: The British Parliament passed a new Act in 1858 and transferred the powers of the East India Company to:
questions.explanation: The Act aimed to ensure a more responsible management of Indian affairs.

questions.text: Under the Act of 1858, the Governor-General of India was given the title of:
questions.explanation: The Viceroy was the personal representative of the British Crown.

questions.text: After the 1857 revolt, the British decided to ________ the proportion of Indian soldiers in the army.
questions.explanation: They decided to recruit more soldiers from among the Gurkhas, Sikhs, and Pathans.

questions.text: Which of these was a major change in policy after 1857?
options: British promised that no more Indian states would be annexed
questions.explanation: Indian rulers were allowed to pass on their kingdoms to their heirs, including adopted sons.

questions.text: The British land policy after 1857 aimed to protect:
questions.explanation: Policies were made to give them security of rights over their lands to win their loyalty.

questions.text: In the context of the rebellion, 'Mutiny' means:
options: When soldiers as a group disobey their officers in the army
questions.explanation: The 1857 revolt was primarily a sepoy mutiny that later became a popular rebellion.

questions.text: Ahmadullah Shah, a maulvi from ________, prophesied that the rule of the British would come to an end soon.
questions.explanation: He came to Lucknow to fight the British and was a popular leader among the people.

questions.text: What was the main reason for the anger among peasants during the 1857 era?
options: High taxes and rigid methods of revenue collection
They wanted to move to England
questions.explanation: Peasants lost their lands when they failed to pay back loans to moneylenders.

questions.text: Why were many Indians suspicious of the railway and telegraph?
options: They were seen as tools for the spread of Western culture and British control
questions.explanation: Every new technology was viewed through the lens of colonial suspicion.

questions.text: Rani Lakshmibai was killed in battle in:
questions.explanation: She died fighting in June 1858.

questions.text: What did the British do to the property of Muslims after the revolt?
options: It was confiscated on a large scale
They distributed it among the poor
questions.explanation: The British held Muslims responsible for the rebellion in a big way and treated them with suspicion.

questions.text: The 1857 revolt is also known as:
questions.explanation: Indian historians often refer to it as the first major attempt to gain freedom.

questions.text: The British were able to suppress the revolt partly because they had:
options: More Indian support than the rebels
The Mughal emperor on their side
questions.explanation: The superior military technology and centralized command gave the British an advantage.

questions.text: What happened to Tantia Tope?
options: He was captured, tried, and killed in 1859
He lived in hiding for 50 years
questions.explanation: He continued to fight a guerrilla war in the jungles of central India before being betrayed.

questions.text: The 'Subsidiary Alliance' and 'Doctrine of Lapse' were causes of:
questions.explanation: These policies led to the loss of territory and dignity for many royal families.

questions.text: Many people in 1857 believed that the British were out to destroy their ________.
questions.explanation: Rumors about bone dust in flour and greased cartridges fueled this fear.

questions.text: Who was the Secretary of State for India established by the Act of 1858?
options: A member of the British Cabinet responsible for Indian affairs
questions.explanation: He was assisted by a council called the India Council.

questions.text: The British promised to respect the ________ of the people of India after 1857.
questions.explanation: They realized that interfering in local customs had contributed to the revolt.

questions.text: Which community did the British particularly avoid recruiting for the army after the revolt?
options: Soldiers from Awadh, Bihar, Central India and South India
questions.explanation: These were the areas where the rebellion had been most intense.

questions.text: What was the 'Great Uprising' of 1857 primarily?
options: A popular rebellion that threatened British rule in India
questions.explanation: It was the most massive resistance to colonialism in the 19th century.

questions.text: The sepoys at Meerut refused to do the army drill with the new cartridges on:
questions.explanation: 85 sepoys were dismissed and sentenced to ten years in jail for this defiance.

questions.text: Which place in Delhi was the center of the rebellion?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort was the residence of the Mughal Emperor, the leader of the rebels.

questions.text: The 'Enfield Rifle' was the cause of the controversy over:
questions.explanation: The cartridges of this new rifle were the immediate spark for the mutiny.

questions.text: The 1857 revolt showed that there was a lack of ________ among the rebel leaders.
questions.explanation: While brave, the lack of a common program or effective coordination led to their defeat.
